pattern: Math optimisation: for a given element value v you need ceil(k/v) copies; picking the best v to minimise (v-1) + (ceil(k/v)-1) increments+duplicates.
hints: You start with [1]. An increment costs 1 op; a duplication costs 1 op and doubles how many copies you have of the current value.
If you end up with element value v and need m copies, the cost is (v-1) increments plus (m-1) duplications. Express m as ceil(k/v) and find the v that minimises total cost.
Iterate v from 1 up to k: for each v the cost is (v-1) + max(0, ceil(k/v)-1); take the minimum over all v.

edge_inputs.description: k=1, sum already satisfied with no ops
k=4, best is value 2 two copies cost 3
k=3, value 2 with 2 copies is cost 2 but 1 copy of 3 is cost 2 too
k=8, value 3 gives 3 copies cost 2+2=4, value 4 gives 2 copies cost 3+1=4, value 2 gives 4 copies cost 1+3=4 - all tied; ensure min returned
prime k where no clean factoring exists

stress.description: random k values up to 100000 to verify optimality
